articles.title: LPG, PNG prices June 22, 2026: Check domestic, commercial cylinder rates in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, other cities
articles.description: LPG, PNG Prices City Wise: The prices of domestic and commercial LPG cylinders remain unchanged on Monday, June 22 after the recent hike on June 7. The OMCs reduced the number of subsidised cooking gas cylinders available annually to beneficiaries of Ujjwala scheme to four on June 8. Domestic cooking gas prices were also increased by Rs 29 per cylinder.

articles.title: Indian equity markets open in green on lower crude prices, favourable global cues
articles.description: Indian equity markets opened in positive territory on Monday, supported by stable global indicators and a notable decline in international crude oil prices.

articles.title: LPG prices today, 22 June: Domestic and commercial cylinder rates in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Kolkata, and other cities
articles.description: LPG cylinder prices remained stable on 22 June, despite previous hikes. The government claims that Indian consumers benefit from some of the lowest cooking gas prices globally, with significant subsidies still in place amid rising international fuel costs.

articles.title: Gold rebounds after two-day fall; silver jumps Rs 4,000/kg on progress in US-Iran peace talks. Should you buy?
articles.description: Gold prices rose on MCX on Monday, snapping a two-day decline after oil prices softened amid signs of progress in U.S.-Iran peace talks. The recovery comes after a sharp selloff in the previous session, though hawkish Federal Reserve signals and expectations of higher U.S. interest rates continue to weigh on the outlook for precious metals.

articles.title: Indian markets open higher; Rupee falls 9 paise, now at 94.42 against US dollar
articles.description: Rupee falls 9 paise to 94.42 against the US dollar in early trade, with investors tracking US-Iran talks and crude oil prices.
